title: Rootless containers not starting
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I am following podman rootless tutorial (https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/tutorials/rootless_tutorial.md) and on fresh arch linux install after following all of the steps I get error when starting container as non-root user.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. fresh arch installation

2. follow https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/tutorials/rootless_tutorial.md

3. podman pull nginx

4. podman run --rm nginx

**Describe the results you received:**
error message:
```
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:109: jailing process inside rootfs caused \\\"permission denied\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
```

With debug enables:

```
time="2020-01-22T05:58:17Z" level=debug msg="Using conmon: \"/usr/bin/conmon\""
time="2020-01-22T05:58:17Z" level=debug msg="Initializing boltdb state at /home/grzegorz/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db"

```

Relevant log from journal:
```
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown systemd[375]: Started podman-694.scope.
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown podman[694]: 2020-01-22 05:58:17.487550857 +0000 UTC m=+0.075041442 container create 1576d57206711ff36165125be5f3f2fda66ace95bbb9cc9ee3f93295db20d39d (image=docker.io/library/nginx:latest, name=stoic_noyce)
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown conmon[709]: conmon 1576d57206711ff36165 <ndebug>: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown conmon[710]: conmon 1576d57206711ff36165 <ninfo>: attach sock path: /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/1576d57206711ff36165125be5f3f2fda66ace95bbb9cc9ee3f93295db20d39d/attach
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown conmon[710]: conmon 1576d57206711ff36165 <ninfo>: addr{sun_family=AF_UNIX, sun_path=/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/1576d57206711ff36165125be5f3f2fda66ace95bbb9cc9ee3f93295db20d39d/attach}

Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown conmon[710]: conmon 1576d57206711ff36165 <ninfo>: winsz read side: 17, winsz write side: 17
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown conmon[710]: conmon 1576d57206711ff36165 <error>: Failed to create container: exit status 1
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown podman[694]: 2020-01-22 05:58:17.624108615 +0000 UTC m=+0.211599207 container remove 1576d57206711ff36165125be5f3f2fda66ace95bbb9cc9ee3f93295db20d39d (image=docker.io/library/nginx:latest, name=stoic_noyce)
Jan 22 05:58:17 unknown systemd[375]: podman-694.scope: Succeeded.
```

I also found that in order to run unprivileged LXC containers on arch following line should be added to `/etc/pam.d/system-login` (but it did not resolve the problem):
```
session optional pam_cgfs.so -c freezer,memory,name=systemd,unified
```

I also tried to follow warning messages about missing directories and created them (but this did not help):

```
sudo mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/pids/libpod_parent
sudo chmod ugo+rw /sys/fs/cgroup/pids/libpod_parent
sudo chmod ugo+rw /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent
sudo mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent
sudo mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/libpod_parent
sudo chmod ugo+rw /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/libpod_parent
```

Tried different options from #1552 :
```
[grzegorz@unknown ~]$ podman run --rm --net=host -ti --privileged --userns=host nginx
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:109: jailing process inside rootfs caused \\\"permission denied\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
[grzegorz@unknown ~]$ podman run --rm -it --net host nginx
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:109: jailing process inside rootfs caused \\\"permission denied\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
[grzegorz@unknown ~]$ podman run --rm -it alpine date
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c9b1b535fdd9 done
Copying config e7d92cdc71 [======================================] 1.5KiB / 1.5KiB
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:109: jailing process inside rootfs caused \\\"permission denied\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
[grzegorz@unknown ~]$ podman run --rm -it --entrypoint bash nginx
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:109: jailing process inside rootfs caused \\\"permission denied\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
[grzegorz@unknown ~]$ podman run --systemd=false --rm -it nginx
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:109: jailing process inside rootfs caused \\\"permission denied\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
```

I also wanted to follow the advice to run `podman mount` and then copy/paste long `conmon` command - this is where I managed to get to:

```
[grzegorz@unknown ~]$ podman create alpine
041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881
[grzegorz@unknown ~]$ podman unshare
[root@unknown ~]# podman mount 041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881
/home/grzegorz/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/acd954ebbd008c3b1594832c4e2635d512a06518c67660c4877ca69228caf110/merged
[root@unknown ~]# /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881 -u 041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881 -r /usr/bin/runc -b /home/grzegorz/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881/userdata -p /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881/userdata/pidfile -l k8s-file:/home/grzegorz/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881/userdata/ctr.log --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --socket-dir-path /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket --log-level debug --syslog -t --conmon-pidfile /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/grzegorz/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/containers --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg debug --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg cgroupfs --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --runtime--exit-command-arg runc --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --storage-opt --exit-command-arg overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg 041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied

[root@unknown ~]# podman unmount 041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881
041f7aafc5395d3a4986d371cfcb934f7dec90c68a6dd26f14c870f71f37a881
[root@unknown ~]# exit
exit
```
And this is what was logged into journal:
```
Jan 22 08:14:51 unknown systemd[386]: Started podman-1991.scope.
Jan 22 08:14:51 unknown systemd[386]: podman-1991.scope: Succeeded.
```

comments.body: OK, looks like the reason is because I'm on cgroups V1 and it seems that rootless containers are possible only when using cgroups V2 (https://podman.io/blogs/2019/10/29/podman-crun-f31.html)

> OK, looks like the reason is because I'm on cgroups V1 and it seems that rootless containers are possible only when using cgroups V2 (https://podman.io/blogs/2019/10/29/podman-crun-f31.html)

This is not true.  Rootless works on v1 and v2.  The underlying tooling does change however.
Yup you are right, I double-checked everything again and it was permissions issue on my host.

@baude @grzegorzk 
I have the exact same error on Debian Buster  - conmon wants to set /proc/self/oom_score_adj to -1 which is not allowed - how to fix this?

Might be the last lines podman is printing are: 
DEBU[0000] ExitCode msg: "sd-bus add match: operation not permitted: oci runtime permission denied error" 
ERRO[0000] sd-bus add match: Operation not permitted: OCI runtime permission denied error
Not sure they are related.  I think the error is not able to communicate with the systemd dbus?
Mhm looks like this one https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4289 but DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS is set and permissions are ok
Log Level debug and even the Journal do not show more infos.

@disaster123 in my case it was a few things. One of them was that due to weirdness how I have built my test system I had wrong permissions on the `/` (0711). Another problem was that I tried to run things while simply `su` as target user rather than logging in as that user.
